GT LEARN #72 🧮 Edit Distance HARD DP
exp: 3

📝 Problem Statement

Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations required to convert word1 to word2. Allowed operations: Insert, Delete, Replace.

🧠 Solving Approach

Use a 2D DP table where dp[i][j] represents the min edits to convert first i chars of word1 to first j chars of word2. Fill it row by row using previous results.

Base Case Empty string to another needs N insertions/deletions.
Match If chars match, cost is 0 (take diagonal).
Mismatch Take min of Insert, Delete, Replace + 1.
Time: O(M × N) Space: O(M × N)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

T1 3 ops
w1="horse" w2="ros"
T2 5 ops
w1="intention" w2="execution"
T3 0 ops
w1="abc" w2="abc"
—
Click a test case or edit inputs and Run to recompute. Step with ▶ to watch the table fill cell-by-cell.
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — DP Table Fill
🧱 DP Table (Min Edits)
Uncomputed Current Cell Neighbors Computed
Small arrows: dashed = 3 candidate predecessors, green + halo = chosen min cost source
⚖️ Operation Logic
Chars: -
Candidates:
-
Chosen: -
✅ Final Result
0/0 INIT
←→ 100%
